key_facts: France relies on nuclear energy for approximately 70% of its electricity generation, operating 56 reactors (as of 2024).
Germany completed its nuclear phase-out on April 15, 2023, shutting down its last three reactors (Emsland, Isar 2, Neckarwestheim 2).
France leads an EU Nuclear Alliance of approximately 14 member states advocating for nuclear energy in decarbonization.
Franco-German bilateral cooperation mechanisms include the Franco-German Council of Ministers and various sectoral working groups on energy.
By late 2024-2025, there were signals of Franco-German rapprochement on energy policy, driven by European energy security concerns.
